title: Fix timeframe propagation bug: Settings selection not reflected in Results screen

requirements.summary: Fix localStorage key mismatch between Settings.tsx and localCandleAnalysis.ts to ensure consistent timeframe storage and retrieval
acceptanceCriteria: The localStorage key used to save the timeframe in Settings.tsx is identical to the key used to read it in localCandleAnalysis.ts
Selecting M5 in Settings persists 'M5' to localStorage under the unified key
Selecting M3 in Settings persists 'M3' to localStorage under the unified key
localCandleAnalysis.ts reads the timeframe at the moment analyzeChartImage() is called, not at import/module load time
The LocalAnalysisResult.timeframe field contains 'M5' when user had M5 selected, 'M3' when M3 was selected, 'M1' when M1 was selected

file_operations.description: Update the timeframe selection handler to write the selected timeframe value ('M1', 'M3', or 'M5') to localStorage using a unified constant key 'settings_timeframe'. Ensure the value is persisted immediately on selection change before calling the updateSettings mutation. Verify that the same key constant is used consistently throughout the file.

Move the timeframe localStorage read operation from module initialization into the analyzeChartImage() function body so it reads the current value at analysis execution time. Use the same unified constant key 'settings_timeframe' to retrieve the timeframe value. Ensure the retrieved timeframe ('M1', 'M3', or 'M5') is correctly assigned to the LocalAnalysisResult.timeframe field. Add a fallback default to 'M1' if no value is found in localStorage.

requirements.summary: Fix Settings page timeframe selector UI state to immediately reflect selected value and persist without reverting
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting M5 in the timeframe selector immediately shows M5 as the active/selected option without reverting
Selecting M3 in the timeframe selector immediately shows M3 as the active/selected option without reverting
The selected value is written to localStorage immediately on change
The updateSettings mutation is called with the new timeframe value
'Configuração salva!' toast appears after successful save
Navigating away and returning to Settings shows the previously saved timeframe value

file_operations.description: Fix the timeframe selector's onChange handler to update the local React state immediately (using setSettings or equivalent state updater) before persisting to localStorage and calling the backend mutation. Ensure the select element's value prop is bound to the current state so it displays the selected option correctly. Add the Portuguese success toast 'Configuração salva!' after the updateSettings mutation completes successfully. Verify that the initial timeframe value is correctly loaded from the backend settings or localStorage on component mount to prevent incorrect initial display.

requirements.summary: Fix countdown timer and Time Frame tile in Results page to use the correct timeframe from analysis result
acceptanceCriteria: When analysis result contains timeframe='M5', the countdown timer starts at 05:00 (300 seconds)
When analysis result contains timeframe='M3', the countdown timer starts at 03:00 (180 seconds)
When analysis result contains timeframe='M1', the countdown timer starts at 01:00 (60 seconds)
The Time Frame tile displays 'M5' when the analysis result timeframe is M5, not 'M1'
The Time Frame tile displays 'M3' when the analysis result timeframe is M3
'Entrar às HH:MM' shows current time + correct timeframe duration (e.g., +5 min for M5)
The Time Frame tile subtitle shows '5 min/vela' for M5, '3 min/vela' for M3, '1 min/vela' for M1

file_operations.description: Read the timeframe value from the analysis result object (result.timeframe) and compute the correct countdown duration based on it: M1 = 60 seconds, M3 = 180 seconds, M5 = 300 seconds. Pass this computed duration to the useCountdownTimer hook. Update the Time Frame tile to display result.timeframe directly instead of any hardcoded or default value. Update the subtitle logic to show '1 min/vela' for M1, '3 min/vela' for M3, and '5 min/vela' for M5 based on result.timeframe. Update the 'Entrar às HH:MM' calculation to add the correct number of minutes to the current time based on the timeframe (1, 3, or 5 minutes).

Ensure the hook accepts the timeframe-derived duration parameter correctly and initializes the countdown timer with it. Verify that the hook returns the formatted time string in MM:SS format and the finished flag as expected by the Results page. No logic changes are required if the hook already accepts a duration in seconds; this operation confirms compatibility.
